titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Yale School of Drama Audio-Visual Recordings
Yale School of Drama Audio-Visual Recordings
titlestmt.author: compiled by staff of Manuscripts and Archives

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday May 29, 2021 at 07:01

did.unittitle: Yale School of Drama audio-visual recordings
did.abstract: The materials consist of audiovisual recordings documenting productions, marketing, presentations, symposia, and events of the Yale School of Drama and the Yale Repertory Theatre.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Forms part of Yale Record Group 22-H (YRG 22-H), Photographs, audiovisual materials, and memorabilia documenting the Yale School of Drama.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: The materials were transferred from the Drama Library, 2006-2008; School of Drama, 2009-2010 and 2013.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy for their personal use should consult Copying Services information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Duplication of media is prohibited to all non-authorized requestors.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Yale School of Drama Audio-Visual Recordings (RU 1024). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Yale University records are arranged and described at the accession level by the creating office. The University Archives creates collection level descriptive records, but typically does no further arrangement and description at the accession level.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The materials are arranged by accession, thereunder by production name.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The materials consist of audiovisual recordings documenting productions, marketing, presentations, symposia, and events of the Yale School of Drama and the Yale Repertory Theatre.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Box 110 of Accession 2007-A-103 contained a video VHS tape, titled "Black Flamingos, 1996 May 3," that was found to be unplayable upon digitization in 2018. It was therefore removed from the collection. Box 190 of Accession 2007-A-103 contained a video VHS tape, titled "Tartuffe, 1996 April 11-13," that was found to be unplayable upon digitization in 2018. It was therefore removed from the collection.

did.unittitle: Audiovisual recordings of productions, presentations, and events

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy for their personal use should consult Copying Services information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access.

did.unittitle: Yale-ABC Conference on drama on TV
Opening dinner (Professors Cole and Davison), broadcast quality
Opening dinner (Professors Cole and Davison), dub
Report of the conference group leaders (David Davison: chairman, Irwin S. Fox, David Susskind, and Jacqueline Babbin)
Report of the conference group leaders (David Davison: chairman, Irwin S. Fox, David Susskind, and Jacqueline Babbin), dub

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access restricted to Yale School of Drama faculty and students only.

Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy for their personal use should consult Copying Services information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site.

title.part: Are You Now or Have You Ever Been

did.unittitle: Use copy for boxes 61-64 and 728, 3 audiocassettes

title.part: Bingo, Scenes of Money and Death

did.unittitle: Death Comes to Us All, Mary Agnes , 3 audiotapes (reel-to-reel)
Use copy for boxes 135-140 and 729, 2 audiocassette
Emlyn Williams as Dylan Thomas Growing Up
Use copy for boxes 523-527 and 738, 3 audiocassettes

title.part: In Good King Charles' Golden Days

odd.head: General
odd.p: Performance ran from December 29-January 13, 1979, University Theatre. Directed by Steve Lawson; Music Direction and Arrangements – Rusty Magee; Choreography – Wesley Fata; Sets – Randy Drake; Costumes – Rita Ryack; Lighting – William M. Armstrong. Cast: Caris Corfman, Warren Manzi, Nancy Mayans, Dave K. Miller, H.S. Murphy, Elizabeth Norment, David Prittie.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives and online.

title.part: Narrow Road to the Deep North
Night is Mother to the Day

did.unittitle: Use copy for boxes432-433 and 732, 2 audiocassettes

title.part: Terry by Terry: Terry Won't Talk
The Tragical History of Dr. Faustus

did.unittitle: The Blood Knot exerpt from Act I with Athol Fugard and Zakes Mokae

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Production with Patrick Magee and Gleda Jackson, RSC.

did.unittitle: Chambers, David and Joel Schecter, "Brecht and Political Theatre," Yale 101 lecture

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A coproduction of Yale Repertory Theatre and Connecticut Public Radio.

did.unittitle: Program 1: Prince, Hal and Lloyd Richards, "Directing the New Play [commercially-produced]
Program 2: Gussow, Mel, Edith Oliver, Michael Feingold, and Malcolm Johnson, "Critics on Their Art [commercially-produced]
Program 3: Martin, Elliot, Liz McCann, and Morton Gottleib, "Process on Broadway [commercially-produced]
Program 4: Pendleton, Austin, Fran Kumin, and Jan Miner, "Casting and Performing [commercially-produced]
Program 5: Guare, John, Stuart Browne, and Harry Kondoleon, "Contemporary Playwrights [commercially-produced]
Demonstration of Character Mask, Class of 1988
An 83 year old woman born in Ireland

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Recording includes Athol Fugard glossaries for Statements , The Blood Knot , Hello and Goodbye , and Boesman and Lena .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Recording includes a reading of character names from Ibsen and Strindberg.

did.unittitle: Draper, Ruth, The Italian Lesson , Doctors and Diets
Grove, Eddy, Lecture on Michael Chekhov's contribution to actor training

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A silent film documenting the backstage preparations for a theatrical performance by Yale School of Drama.

did.unittitle: Chekhov's Names and Uncle Vanya Names and Places in Depth
Fugard, Athol, My Children! My Africa!

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Words and names read by Athol Fugard.

Shakespeare, William, Henry IV Part I
Shakespeare, William, The Merchant of Venice
Safety Conference, Yale School of Drama: Fire Codes and Fire Safety

Wilder, Isabel, interview by Sara Low on experiences as member of Yale School Drama class of 1928
Season IV. Dumang, Wasserstein, and Weaver
Tipton, Jennifer, Tharon Musser, and Ming Cho Lee (mod.), "Telling Stories Through Light"
Morrison, Toni, August Wilson, and Robert Stepto (mod.), "The Poetry of Storytelling"
Simms, Laura, "A Concert of Wintertales," audiocassette
Brever, Lee, Richard Foreman, and Gitta Honegger (mod.), "The Art of Storytelling"
Kondoleon, Harry, Wendy Wasserstein, and Thomas Balse (mod.), "How I Got That Story"
Guare, John and Gregory Mosher, "Bringing Stories to Life"
Yale School of Drama Faculty and Buildings, Costume Shop
Elvira, Mistress of the Dark, Fright Sound Tape [commercially-produced]
Vol. VI: "From Verona to Denmark: A Poetic Transition [commercially-produced]
Vol. XIV: "The Way It Came Together [commercially-produced]
